Government Notification, Revenue & Forest Department No.WLP- 12.20/प R.No.245 / F-1, dated
Letter No.23 (2) / from the Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (Wildlife), Maharashtra State, Nagpur
Wazi / Survey / Q. 113/223, Date 28.04.2022.
3. Letter No. from the Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (Wildlife), Maharashtra State, Nagpur: Cell-23 (2) /
The Proposal: -

TRAM has declared an area of 225.24 sq km in the Kolhapur Forest Circle, Chandgarh and Patna Forest Range of Kolhapur Forest Division as Chandgarh Conservation Reserve as per the notification in Reference No. 1 above. The government was considering setting up a Chandgad-Patna Conservation Reserve Management Committee to manage the area and advise the Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (Wildlife) and Chief Wildlife Warden, Maharashtra State, Nagpur.
The Government's Decisions:

The Chandgarh Conservation Reserve Management Committee is being constituted under the provisions of Section 36B (1) and (2) of the Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972 for the management of the Chandgarh Conservation Reserve as follows: -
table_trans: Sarpanch Gram Panchayat, Pilani and Kanur Khud

para_trans: The functions of the aforesaid Management Committee shall be as follows: -
The working of the committee would be limited to the Chandgad Conservation Reserve.
The 2.सदर Committee on Proposals and Suggestions for the Protection and Management of Conservation Reserves
The Wildlife Warden will send them and provide the necessary support.
3. The Wildlife (Protection) Act, 1972 and other forest related Acts in the Conservation Reserves and
The committee will provide guidance on whether the laws will not be violated.
The term of office of the Committee shall be for a period of 3 years from the date of issue of this Government Decision.
This Government Decision is available on the website of the Government of Maharashtra Www.maharashtra.gov.in.
